Event: Logan introduces Greg as "Cousin Craig"
Explanation: Minor status harm to Greg while amusing/establishing Logan’s dominance with no cost to himself.

Event: Waystar records Greg as "Greg Roy"
Explanation: Misnaming slightly helps admin continuity while erasing Greg’s identity/status.

Event: Mattson derisively calls Greg "Gary"
Explanation: He asserts superiority at Greg’s expense, minor gain in social dominance.

Event: Greg mockingly asks Logan "Where are your kids?"
Explanation: Greg vents and tests power but provokes a nasty outburst, harming Logan more.

Event: Greg decides to side with Kendall because Kendall was nice to him
Explanation: Aligning with a more supportive ally helps Greg while aiding Kendall’s faction.

Event: Greg betrays Tom’s plan to go public with cruise scandal to Gerri
Explanation: He boosts his credibility and protection by undermining Tom’s moral move.

Event: Gerri believes Greg over Shiv about Tom’s plan
Explanation: Gerri gains leverage using Greg’s info, at Shiv’s expense.

Event: Greg worries about the damning cruise files all season
Explanation: Anxiety harms him personally but stems from wanting wrongdoing addressed.

Event: Greg secretly saves a few cruise-scandal files from Tom’s destruction
Explanation: He protects leverage for himself more than victims, while endangering the company.

Event: Tom orders Greg to destroy cruise-scandal documents
Explanation: Protects Tom and the firm short-term while harming justice and victims.

Event: Greg sides with Kendall and releases the records
Explanation: He boosts his own position and potential inheritance while causing major harm to the firm.

Event: Greg factors in regaining inheritance as motive for releasing records
Explanation: He uses moral action partly as leverage to reclaim money from his grandfather.

Event: Greg blackmails Tom repeatedly
Explanation: He secures protection and advantage while hurting Tom’s autonomy.

Event: Greg blackmails multiple other characters who underestimate him
Explanation: Systematically gains leverage by threatening others.

Event: Tom abuses his authority over Greg at work
Explanation: He asserts dominance/enjoyment while degrading Greg’s position and well-being.

Event: Tom has Greg do dirty work like shredding docs after long drive
Explanation: He offloads stress and liability to Greg, who suffers tedium and risk.

Event: Greg eagerly declares he doesn’t care about others and mocks needing a soul
Explanation: Cynical stance helps him operate ruthlessly while normalizing harm to others.

Event: Greg betrays anyone when it serves his interests
Explanation: Pattern of opportunistic betrayal maximizes his gain at others’ expense.

Event: Greg fixates on “attack child” invading the safe room
Explanation: Irrational anxiety harms him while not affecting others.

Event: Tom drags Greg into the cruise crimes cover-up
Explanation: Makes Greg complicit, protecting Tom while exposing Greg to legal risk.

Event: Greg rats Tom out to Gerri over cruise crimes to protect his job
Explanation: He shields himself by sacrificing Tom’s attempt to come clean.

Event: Greg initially tries to remain morally pure in the family’s world
Explanation: Idealism hinders his advancement while providing a thin moral counterweight.

Event: Greg sues his grandfather’s charity to reclaim inheritance
Explanation: He chases money via litigation that directly harms the charity’s resources.

Event: Greg dumps Comfry to pursue a wealthy contessa
Explanation: He increases social/financial prospects at the cost of Comfry’s feelings.

Event: Greg accepts Tom’s “deal with the devil” to stay at Waystar
Explanation: He chooses career security over his conscience.

Event: Greg gets a haircut because he wants someone soothing to touch his head
Explanation: Buys comfort and human contact, mildly benefits barber via business.

Event: Greg tries to stay on everyone’s good side to keep his wealth access
Explanation: People-pleasing and manipulation protect his position more than theirs.

Event: Greg flip-flops three times on handling the cruise scandal for pragmatism
Explanation: He navigates to personally safest outcome while worsening others’ uncertainty.

Event: Greg rats Tom out to Gerri when Tom wants to come clean
Explanation: He protects himself at the direct cost of Tom’s plan and exposure.

Event: Greg sides with Kendall to take down Logan using hidden evidence
Explanation: He bets on Kendall’s rise, damaging Logan and the company.

Event: Greg defects back to Logan when Kendall’s route seems impractical
Explanation: He abandons Kendall to align with more secure power.

Event: Greg sleeps with a ditzy woman in Logan’s home
Explanation: He gains casual sex and status-y thrill with little concern for her safety.

Event: Greg confesses the hookup and blames the woman, letting Colin “deal with” her
Explanation: He shifts all blame and risk to her to preserve his standing.

Event: Greg delivers bad news to Kerry about not appearing on ATN
Explanation: He safeguards Tom’s position and his own role while hurting Kerry’s ambitions.

Event: Greg piles on Kerry only after Marcia has emotionally destroyed her
Explanation: He gains status with higher-ups by safely bullying someone already crushed.

Event: Shiv threatens to “screw” Greg if he follows Tom’s orders in “America Decides”
Explanation: She tries to intimidate him to protect her own secrets.

Event: Greg leaks Shiv’s dealings with Matsson to Kendall to spite her
Explanation: He vindictively boosts his standing with Kendall at Shiv’s expense.

Event: Greg’s father abandons the family to pursue his own life
Explanation: He gains personal freedom while harming familial bonds and Greg.

Event: Ewan cuts Greg out of his will after Greg twice sides with Logan
Explanation: He tries to teach Greg a lesson but mostly weakens Greg and his own influence.

Event: Greg ignores Ewan’s advice and tries to sue his grandfather
Explanation: He doubles down on greed, further alienating family and harming charity.

Event: Tom uses Greg as a footstool/human furniture
Explanation: He asserts dominance and amusement by humiliating Greg.

Event: Greg tries to get family to call him “Gregory”
Explanation: He attempts a more dignified image with negligible effect on others.

Event: Greg gets fired from mascot job after getting high before a shift
Explanation: He loses employment while slightly amusing customers/colleagues.

Event: Greg unsuccessfully blames his firing on a contact high
Explanation: Dishonesty fails, further damaging credibility without helping others.

Event: Logan offers Greg a drink from his bar
Explanation: Small gesture builds rapport, modestly benefits both.

Event: Greg asks for a rum and coke at Logan’s bar
Explanation: Minor comfort for himself, neutral-to-mildly amusing for Logan.

Event: Greg questions the safety of the “safe room” design
Explanation: He correctly identifies flaws, potentially improving awareness.

Event: Greg goes around Ewan to seek other legal help
Explanation: He looks for counsel centered on his own interests, undermining Ewan’s guidance.

Event: Greg leverages Roys’ secrets and low status to advance
Explanation: He uses information asymmetry to climb, often harming those he exposes.

Event: Tom forces Greg to pull Kerry from ATN consideration
Explanation: He protects himself by delegating a cruel task that harms Kerry.

Event: Greg spends time hanging out with waiter Andrew before Shiv’s wedding
Explanation: Mutual socialization; Greg gains company and Andrew gets inclusion.

Event: Greg reassures a performer in Kendall’s “compliment tunnel” after Tom snipes
Explanation: He gives emotional support at little cost to himself.

Event: Greg deals drugs to Kendall and others while also using them himself
Explanation: He endangers his own health and legal status while enabling others’ habits.

Event: Greg gradually abandons his principles to fit into Waystar
Explanation: He increases his power/income while contributing to a toxic culture.

Event: Greg accurately calls Logan paranoid and vindictive
Explanation: Correct assessment can guide safer strategic choices.

Event: Greg notes Roman is a sex pest and Tom abuses subordinates
Explanation: Recognizing others’ flaws helps him navigate dangers, possibly warn others.

Event: Greg allows himself to be pressed and guilted into almost anything
Explanation: His capitulation harms his autonomy while helping users of him.

Event: Greg learns to stand up more to Tom’s abuse
Explanation: He improves his own position while only modestly inconveniencing Tom.

Event: Greg slides into bullying others when safe
Explanation: He strengthens his standing by punching down.

Event: Greg outlines how he “jackknifes in and slits throats” when firing
Explanation: He embraces ruthless HR role for advancement, hurting employees.

Event: Greg says HR picked him because he looks like he cares but doesn’t
Explanation: He weaponizes a caring image to execute harmful decisions.

Event: Greg rises into Inhuman Resources, coldly laying off people
Explanation: He gains career power through mass firings.

Event: Greg becomes Morality Pet for Logan by talking candidly with him
Explanation: His presence slightly humanizes Logan while giving Greg protection.

Event: Greg opportunistically switches allegiance from Tom to Kendall
Explanation: He trades loyalties to improve his prospects, undermining Tom.

Event: Greg flips back again whenever a better patron appears
Explanation: Constant side-switching maximizes self-gain, destabilizing others.

Event: Greg leverages cousin status for sex and privileges
Explanation: He extracts personal benefits through status-based entitlement.

Event: Greg gathers intel and leaks it to whichever side suits him
Explanation: He uses information as currency, often at others’ expense.

Event: Greg uses blackmail on Tom and still feels ashamed by Tom’s praise
Explanation: He suffers inner conflict despite successfully protecting himself.
